channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Russia hits Kyiv with drones and ballistic missiles, killing 1 and injuring 31
item.description: Russia has launched a mass drone and missile attack on Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, killing one person and injuring at least 31. Local authorities report damage across six districts.
item.content:encoded:
Russia has launched a mass drone and missile attack on Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, killing one person and injuring at least 31. Local authorities report damage across six districts.

(Image credit: Evgeniy Maloletka)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Remains of 2nd U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
item.description: Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.
item.content:encoded:
Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.

(Image credit: Mosa'ab Elshamy)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What's at stake for the people of Thitu Island as U.S. and Chinese leaders meet
item.description: As U.S. and Chinese leaders meet this week, the world is watching, and so are residents of Thitu Island in the disputed South China Sea, known in the Philippines as the West Philippine Sea.
item.content:encoded: As U.S. and Chinese leaders meet this week, the world is watching, and so are residents of Thitu Island in the disputed South China Sea, known in the Philippines as the West Philippine Sea.
item.dc:creator: Jan Camenzind Broomby

item.title: A severe mouse plague puts Australian crops in danger
item.description: Australia's grain farmers, already strained by war-driven shortages, now face a severe mouse plague that could devastate crops in a key growing region.
item.content:encoded: Australia's grain farmers, already strained by war-driven shortages, now face a severe mouse plague that could devastate crops in a key growing region.
item.dc:creator: Kristina Kukolja

item.title: In Hormuz deadlock, U.S. and Iran refuse to back down
item.description: The U.S. and Iran remain deadlocked in the Strait of Hormuz, with no clear way forward.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Iran remain deadlocked in the Strait of Hormuz, with no clear way forward.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: Five countries boycott Eurovision Song Contest over Israel's participation
item.description: Israel's participation in the Eurovision song contest once again stirs controversy — and a walkout by five countries.
item.content:encoded: Israel's participation in the Eurovision song contest once again stirs controversy — and a walkout by five countries.
item.dc:creator: Itay Stern

item.title: Greetings from Seville, where springtime means caracoles
item.description: Spring is snail season in Seville. Caracoles in southern Spain differ from the well-known French escargot — they're smaller and eaten directly from the shell. And everyone has a favorite tapas bar that serves them.
item.content:encoded: Spring is snail season in Seville. Caracoles in southern Spain differ from the well-known French escargot — they're smaller and eaten directly from the shell. And everyone has a favorite tapas bar that serves them.
item.dc:creator: Miguel Macias

item.title: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate as police try to arrest senator
item.description: Witnesses say a burst of gunfire has rung out in the Philippine Senate where authorities have tried to arrest a senator who is wanted by the ICC.
item.content:encoded:
Witnesses say a burst of gunfire has rung out in the Philippine Senate where authorities have tried to arrest a senator who is wanted by the ICC.

(Image credit: Aaron Favila)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: War in Iran costs $29 billion so far. And, students are finally improving in math
item.description: Pentagon officials told Congress they estimate the war in Iran has cost $29 billion so far. And, a new Education Scorecard shows U.S. students are improving in math.
item.content:encoded:
Pentagon officials told Congress they estimate the war in Iran has cost $29 billion so far. And, a new Education Scorecard shows U.S. students are improving in math.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump expected to talk Iran and trade during summit in China
item.description: President Trump arrives in China Wednesday for a summit with his Chinese counterpart, where the war in Iran, trade policies and Taiwan are expected to be major points of discussion.
item.content:encoded: President Trump arrives in China Wednesday for a summit with his Chinese counterpart, where the war in Iran, trade policies and Taiwan are expected to be major points of discussion.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak
